narrative: Dear [Student's Name], as a white male applicant with an upper-middle-class family background, you've put significant effort into your law school application, including your undergraduate GPA of 2.70, LSAT score of 32, and solid Year 1 school decile ranking of 6th—above the typical student's 5.71 average. However, your Year 3 decile dropped sharply to 1st, well below the average of about 5.53, and you're planning to attend part-time, which is less common as most students go full-time. The admissions model, which predicts first-time bar exam success based on profiles like yours, estimated a 43% chance you'll fail the bar—high enough to trigger a rejection to protect the school's outcomes. The biggest factor pushing toward this failure prediction was your sharp drop in Year 3 performance, as it signaled potential challenges in sustaining academic momentum compared to peers who held steady or improved. On a brighter note, your stronger Year 1 decile worked strongly in your favor, pulling the prediction toward passing, much like it does for top early performers in the dataset. Your undergraduate GPA and part-time status also nudged things positively, showing some resilience despite being below averages, while your LSAT and family income provided smaller boosts toward success. Overall, your profile stands out from typical admitted students—who often maintain higher deciles throughout, score around 36.7 on the LSAT, and go full-time—making the model cautious about your bar exam odds. This isn't a judgment on your potential as a lawyer, but a data-driven call highlighting that Year 3 dip as the key hurdle. To strengthen future applications, focus on boosting your academic deciles, perhaps through targeted study habits or additional coursework, and consider full-time options if feasible. Retaking the LSAT for a higher score could also shift your profile closer to successful peers. You're clearly capable, given your Year 1 success—keep building on that, and doors will open. We believe in your ability to turn this around.
